pr-review.architecture: Reviews whether the diff introduces architectural issues such as broken module boundaries, inconsistent component APIs, poor abstraction choices, or hidden coupling.
pr-review.behavior: Reviews whether the diff can fail at runtime, produce incorrect behavior, or silently break existing callers through logic errors, broken execution paths, or contract mismatches.
pr-review.type-safety: Reviews whether the diff weakens TypeScript correctness or public API safety through invalid types, unsafe assertions, missing exports, or broken polymorphic typing.
pr-review.security: Reviews whether the diff introduces security or error-handling issues at trust boundaries, in data handling, dependency choices, or failure paths.
pr-review.test-coverage: Reviews whether the diff leaves specific bug-prone behaviors insufficiently tested or contains weak tests that would fail to catch concrete regressions.
pr-review.spec-conformance: Reviews whether the diff actually implements the referenced ticket, acceptance criteria, and stated PR goals without missing requirements or adding undocumented scope.

spec-review.completeness: Reviews whether the spec document is coherent as a whole, states the problem clearly, explains trade-offs, provides enough structure and success criteria, and covers all major components, interfaces, failure paths, and implementation-critical sections.
spec-review.security: Reviews whether the spec document defines trust boundaries, authentication, authorization, data protection, secrets handling, and other core security controls before implementation.
spec-review.scope: Reviews whether the spec document is appropriately scoped for the stated problem and avoids speculative complexity, unjustified abstractions, or premature future-proofing.
spec-review.api-design: Reviews whether the spec document specifies workable API contracts, error semantics, idempotency, pagination, auth requirements, and other consumer-facing behaviors.
spec-review.data-modeling: Reviews whether the spec document defines sound schemas, data flows, consistency guarantees, ownership boundaries, indexing, and lifecycle policies.
spec-review.consistency: Reviews whether the spec document contradicts itself across sections, reuses terms inconsistently, or gives implementers conflicting constraints or responsibilities.
spec-review.accessibility: Reviews whether the spec document treats accessibility as a first-class requirement across semantics, keyboard support, focus handling, screen readers, motion, and alternative representations.
spec-review.test-plan: Reviews whether the spec document includes an adequate test strategy covering major behaviors, failure paths, environments, non-functional needs, and observability validation.

plan-review.completeness: Reviews whether the implementation plan is coherent overall, covers the full end-to-end execution path including prerequisites, verification steps, rollback paths, cleanup, data handling, and communications.
plan-review.security: Reviews whether the implementation plan addresses identity lifecycle, least privilege, secrets management, trust boundaries, encryption, auditability, and pre-launch security validation.
plan-review.sequencing: Reviews whether the implementation plan orders dependent steps correctly, accounts for implicit prerequisites and propagation delays, and handles dual-write or multi-system coordination safely.
plan-review.viability: Reviews whether the implementation plan is actually executable with valid commands, realistic timing, and idempotent steps, and whether it identifies realistic failure modes, quantifies blast radius, and defines mitigations.
